summary: Low-risk mutual NDA. Well-drafted by experienced counsel — all 10 clauses present with playbook-compliant positions. English law with English courts. 3-year term. Standard of care is 'same degree of care as own confidential information.' Two minor observations noted but neither rises to the level of a required amendment: (1) the return/destruction period is 14 days vs our preferred 10 — not material; (2) the third-party disclosure exclusion requires the disclosing party to confirm in writing that disclosure is authorised — slightly elevated burden but provides additional protection for us as potential disclosing party. Recommend Legal Manager sign-off with no redlines required.

playbook_results.deviation: Well-defined. Written information marked 'Confidential.' Oral information confirmed in writing within 10 days. Standard scope — neither too broad nor too narrow.
'Same degree of care as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information of like nature, but in no event less than reasonable care.' Exact playbook-required standard.
3 years from Effective Date. No auto-renewal. Obligations survive termination for the stated period only. Within playbook acceptable range.
Compelled disclosure carve-out present. Prior written notice to disclosing party where legally permissible. Disclosure limited to minimum legally required. Acceptable.
14-day return/destruction period (playbook: 10 days). Written certification of destruction required. Minor deviation — not material given the 4-day difference.
Mutual right to seek injunctive relief and other equitable remedies. No pre-consent to injunctive relief without bond. Both parties on equal footing.
English law. Exclusive jurisdiction of the English courts. Preferred playbook jurisdiction. Acceptable.
Genuinely mutual. Identical obligations, rights, and standards apply to both parties. Verified symmetry in sections 3.1 and 3.2.

All four standard exclusions present. Minor: third-party disclosure exclusion requires written confirmation from disclosing party that the third party had authority to disclose. Slightly elevated proof burden but protects both parties.

Explicit no-license clause present in section 8. Clear and unambiguous — 'Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to grant either party any rights, title, or licence in any Confidential Information of the other party.'
